corpus_source: Whiteside, M. — «Python в задачах и упражнениях», ДМК Пресс 2025 (рус. перевод 'Programming Puzzles, Python Edition' 2023). Pages 12–38.

what_is_real_vs_constructed.corpus_pages: REAL — extracted from actual PDF pp.12-38 of Whiteside puzzlebook.
what_is_real_vs_constructed.chunk_content: PARAPHRASED — chunks are functional reconstructions of source sections in own words, with verbatim retention of function signatures, library names, version numbers, URLs, and other facts. Not direct PDF text dump (copyright + extraction quality concerns).
what_is_real_vs_constructed.chunk_ids_and_boundaries: CONSTRUCTED — boundary decisions and ID scheme are mine; not output of a real chunking pipeline.
what_is_real_vs_constructed.questions: CONSTRUCTED from real content — derived from material in pp.12-38, formulated by me; not from external benchmark source.
what_is_real_vs_constructed.runtime_metrics_jsonl: SIMULATED — top-k ordering and fit_status are my plausible estimates of what a baseline retrieval system might return. No real retrieval system was run.
what_is_real_vs_constructed.audit_labels: MANUAL — by claude-opus-4-7 as LLM single rater.

simulation_notes: Chunks now anchored in real source material (improvement over previous fluent35 synthetic test bed where chunks were also constructed). Confirmation bias on the 'rater self-constructed chunks' axis is reduced but not eliminated: rater still constructed questions and simulated run. Use this manifest only to validate audit_v0 schema works on real material; do not use for any gate decision or before/after comparison.
notes: Pilot v0.2.1 — Q-known-hit only (Q2 = pygame/PythonTurtle pip install). Q22 (not_in_corpus test for книга publication date) and Q7 (ambiguity test for who) are deferred — protocol §12 stop-rule applies: review pilot 1 before proceeding.
